steps.title: Identify the key logical relationship
steps.content:
The passage states that Harris's poem “suggests the fluid interplay of poetry and painting” through his creative translation of visual imagery into figurative language.

It then introduces that the poem “highlights fundamental tensions between these modes of representation.” The key signal: these are two observations about what the poem does—it reveals both how poetry and painting can work together AND how they fundamentally differ.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
”At the same time” correctly signals that both observations are true simultaneously about Harris's poem.

The poem demonstrates interplay through creative translation, while also revealing fundamental tensions between the art forms. “In other words” falsely suggests restatement, “Therefore” incorrectly implies causation, and “Specifically” treats tensions as an example of interplay.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
